Musical artist

**Benjamin Charles Harper**[[1]](#cite_note-1) (born October 28, 1969) is an American singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) Harper plays an eclectic mix of [blues](/wiki/Blues "Blues"), [folk](/wiki/Folk_music "Folk music"), [soul](/wiki/Soul_music "Soul music"), [reggae](/wiki/Reggae "Reggae"), and rock music,[[3]](#cite_note-3) and he is known for his guitar-playing skills, vocals, live performances, and activism.[[4]](#cite_note-4) He has released twelve studio albums, mostly through [Virgin Records](/wiki/Virgin_Records "Virgin Records"), and has toured internationally.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-:1-6)

Harper is a three-time [Grammy Award](/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award") winner and seven-time nominee, with awards for [Best Pop Instrumental Performance](/wiki/Best_Pop_Instrumental_Performance "Best Pop Instrumental Performance") and [Best Traditional Soul Gospel Album](/wiki/Best_Traditional_Soul_Gospel_Album "Best Traditional Soul Gospel Album") in 2004 and [Best Blues Album](/wiki/Best_Blues_Album "Best Blues Album") in 2013.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

At the 40th [Blues Music Awards](/wiki/Blues_Music_Award "Blues Music Award") ceremony, Harper's joint composition with [Charlie Musselwhite](/wiki/Charlie_Musselwhite "Charlie Musselwhite"), "No Mercy in This Land", was named Song of the Year.[[8]](#cite_note-auto-8)

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ben_Harper&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Harper was born in [Pomona, California](/wiki/Pomona,_California "Pomona, California").[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) His late father, Leonard Harper, was of African-American ancestry, and his mother, Ellen Harper Verdries (née Chase),[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) is Jewish. His maternal great-grandmother was a [Russian](/wiki/Russian_Jewish "Russian Jewish")-[Lithuanian Jew](/wiki/Lithuanian_Jew "Lithuanian Jew").[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13) His parents divorced when he was five years old, and he grew up with his mother's family. Harper has two younger brothers, Joel and [Peter Harper](/wiki/Peter_Harper_(American_artist) "Peter Harper (American artist)").[[14]](#cite_note-14)

Harper began playing guitar as a child.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16) His maternal grandparents' music store, the Folk Music Center and Museum (Claremont, CA), laid a foundation of folk and blues for the artist, complemented by regular patrons [Leonard Cohen](/wiki/Leonard_Cohen "Leonard Cohen"), [Taj Mahal](/wiki/Taj_Mahal_(musician) "Taj Mahal (musician)"), [John Darnielle](/wiki/John_Darnielle "John Darnielle"), and [David Lindley](/wiki/David_Lindley_(musician) "David Lindley (musician)"), and quotes of [William Shakespeare](/wiki/William_Shakespeare "William Shakespeare") and [Robert Frost](/wiki/Robert_Frost "Robert Frost") made often by his grandfather.[[17]](#cite_note-Appleford-17): 5

In 1978, at the age of 9, Harper attended [Bob Marley](/wiki/Bob_Marley "Bob Marley")'s performance in [Burbank, California](/wiki/Burbank,_California "Burbank, California"), where Marley was joined by former bandmate [Peter Tosh](/wiki/Peter_Tosh "Peter Tosh") for the encore. It was, according to Harper, an important influence.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ben_Harper&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

At the age of twelve, Harper played his first gig.[[19]](#cite_note-19) During the 1980s, in his teen years, Harper began to play the [slide guitar](/wiki/Slide_guitar "Slide guitar"), mimicking the style of [Robert Johnson](/wiki/Robert_Johnson_(musician) "Robert Johnson (musician)"). Harper refined his style, taking up the [Weissenborn](/wiki/Weissenborn "Weissenborn") slide guitar.[[17]](#cite_note-Appleford-17): 11 Harper was offered an invitation by [Taj Mahal](/wiki/Taj_Mahal_(musician) "Taj Mahal (musician)") to tour with the artist. They recorded Taj Mahal's album *[Follow the Drinking Gourd](/wiki/Follow_the_Drinkin%27_Gourd "Follow the Drinkin' Gourd")*, released in November 1990, and toured Hawaii.[[17]](#cite_note-Appleford-17)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/42/Ben_Harper_FIJM_2003.jpg/250px-Ben_Harper_FIJM_2003.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ben_Harper_FIJM_2003.jpg)

Harper on stage in 2003

In 1992, Harper recorded the [LP](/wiki/Gramophone_record "Gramophone record") *Pleasure and Pain* with [folk](/wiki/Traditional_folk_music "Traditional folk music") multi-instrumentalist [Tom Freund](/wiki/Tom_Freund "Tom Freund"). After this limited-edition record, Harper secured a lifetime record deal with [Virgin Records](/wiki/Virgin_Records "Virgin Records").[[20]](#cite_note-20) In 1993, he toured venues in California and France with his band, Ben Harper and the Innocent Criminals, and performed with Taj Majal at [Austin City Limits](/wiki/Austin_City_Limits "Austin City Limits").[[21]](#cite_note-1993-tour-21)

In 1994, Harper released his debut album, *[Welcome to the Cruel World](/wiki/Welcome_to_the_Cruel_World "Welcome to the Cruel World")* and toured the US and Canada with the Innocent Criminals.[[22]](#cite_note-1994-tour-22) In November of the same year, he performed two solo shows in Spain.[[22]](#cite_note-1994-tour-22)

He released his second album, *[Fight For Your Mind](/wiki/Fight_For_Your_Mind "Fight For Your Mind")*, in 1995, with Juan Nelson on bass, which became a [college radio](/wiki/College_radio "College radio") favorite and included several songs that Harper has played live throughout his career.[[23]](#cite_note-23) The same year, the band expanded their reach to Europe and performed in the Netherlands, Switzerland, Italy, Spain, Canada, and a handful of US dates.[[24]](#cite_note-24) His music received a great deal of airplay and critical acclaim in Europe and was widely played in Australia (first on [Triple J](/wiki/Triple_J "Triple J") radio).

In 1999, at the [Santa Barbara Bowl](/wiki/Santa_Barbara_Bowl "Santa Barbara Bowl"), Harper met [Jack Johnson](/wiki/Jack_Johnson_(musician) "Jack Johnson (musician)") and sent a demo tape of Johnson's songs to his producer, J. P. Plunier, who then produced Johnson's first album, *[Brushfire Fairytales](/wiki/Brushfire_Fairytales "Brushfire Fairytales")*, in December 2000.[[25]](#cite_note-25) Jack Johnson became the opening act in late February 2001 for the last 23 cities of Ben Harper's "Innocent Criminals" tour of the United States.[[26]](#cite_note-interviews-26)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f7/Ben-harper-toots-and-the-maytals-jack-johnson.jpg/250px-Ben-harper-toots-and-the-maytals-jack-johnson.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ben-harper-toots-and-the-maytals-jack-johnson.jpg)

Ben Harper backstage with [Toots and the Maytals](/wiki/Toots_and_the_Maytals "Toots and the Maytals") and [Jack Johnson](/wiki/Jack_Johnson_(musician) "Jack Johnson (musician)").

In 2002, Harper was one of the featured singers covering Motown hits by [Marvin Gaye](/wiki/Marvin_Gaye "Marvin Gaye") in the documentary, *[Standing in the Shadows of Motown](/wiki/Standing_in_the_Shadows_of_Motown "Standing in the Shadows of Motown")* (a history of [The Funk Brothers](/wiki/The_Funk_Brothers "The Funk Brothers")). Harper was featured on the album [*True Love*](/wiki/True_Love_(Toots_%26_the_Maytals_album) "True Love (Toots & the Maytals album)") by [Toots and the Maytals](/wiki/Toots_and_the_Maytals "Toots and the Maytals"), which won the [Grammy Award](/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award") in 2004 for Best Reggae Album, and showcased many notable musicians including [Willie Nelson](/wiki/Willie_Nelson "Willie Nelson"), [Eric Clapton](/wiki/Eric_Clapton "Eric Clapton"), [Jeff Beck](/wiki/Jeff_Beck "Jeff Beck"), [Trey Anastasio](/wiki/Trey_Anastasio "Trey Anastasio"), [Gwen Stefani](/wiki/Gwen_Stefani "Gwen Stefani") / [No Doubt](/wiki/No_Doubt "No Doubt"), [Bonnie Raitt](/wiki/Bonnie_Raitt "Bonnie Raitt"), [Manu Chao](/wiki/Manu_Chao "Manu Chao"), [The Roots](/wiki/The_Roots "The Roots"), [Ryan Adams](/wiki/Ryan_Adams "Ryan Adams"), [Keith Richards](/wiki/Keith_Richards "Keith Richards"), [Toots Hibbert](/wiki/Toots_Hibbert "Toots Hibbert"), [Paul Douglas](/wiki/Paul_Douglas_(musician) "Paul Douglas (musician)"), Jackie Jackson, [Ken Boothe](/wiki/Ken_Boothe "Ken Boothe"), and [The Skatalites](/wiki/The_Skatalites "The Skatalites").[[27]](#cite_note-27) In 2003, Harper was French [*Rolling Stone* magazine](/wiki/Rolling_Stone_(magazine) "Rolling Stone (magazine)")'s Artist of the Year (*Artiste De L'Année*),[[28]](#cite_note-28) and his Australian tour that year for *[Diamonds on the Inside](/wiki/Diamonds_on_the_Inside "Diamonds on the Inside")* was highly successful with record sales. On April 3, 2004, Harper and [Jack Johnson](/wiki/Jack_Johnson_(musician) "Jack Johnson (musician)") performed with [Toots and the Maytals](/wiki/Toots_and_the_Maytals "Toots and the Maytals") on [*Saturday Night Live* (season 29, episode 16)](/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live_(season_29) "Saturday Night Live (season 29)"), a show hosted by [Donald Trump](/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump").[[29]](#cite_note-29)[[30]](#cite_note-30)

In October 2004, Harper participated in the [Vote for Change](/wiki/Vote_for_Change "Vote for Change") concert tour organized to benefit [Moveon.org](/wiki/Moveon.org "Moveon.org") and encourage people in the [swing states](/wiki/Swing_states "Swing states") to vote during the [2004 U.S. presidential election](/wiki/U.S._presidential_election,_2004 "U.S. presidential election, 2004"). In the same month, Harper contributed a live recording of the song "Oppression" to *[For The Lady](/wiki/For_The_Lady "For The Lady")*, a benefit album for jailed [Nobel Peace Prize](/wiki/Nobel_Peace_Prize "Nobel Peace Prize") winner and Burmese pro-democracy advocate [Aung San Suu Kyi](/wiki/Aung_San_Suu_Kyi "Aung San Suu Kyi").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f6/BenHarper.jpg/250px-BenHarper.jpg)](/wiki/File:BenHarper.jpg)

Harper playing a [Weissenborn](/wiki/Weissenborn "Weissenborn") in [Massey Hall](/wiki/Massey_Hall "Massey Hall"), Toronto, Ontario, 2008

In 2006, Harper released the double album *[Both Sides of the Gun](/wiki/Both_Sides_of_the_Gun "Both Sides of the Gun")* which debuted at number 7 on the [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"). Though uncredited, he appears briefly in the 2006 [David Lynch](/wiki/David_Lynch "David Lynch") film *[Inland Empire](/wiki/Inland_Empire_(film) "Inland Empire (film)")*, alongside his wife [Laura Dern](/wiki/Laura_Dern "Laura Dern").

Harper is part of the [No Nukes group](/wiki/No_Nukes_group "No Nukes group"), which is against the expansion of [nuclear power](/wiki/Nuclear_power "Nuclear power"). In 2007, the group recorded a music video of a version of the [Buffalo Springfield](/wiki/Buffalo_Springfield "Buffalo Springfield") song "[For What It's Worth](/wiki/For_What_It%27s_Worth_(Buffalo_Springfield_song) "For What It's Worth (Buffalo Springfield song)")".[[31]](#cite_note-31)[[32]](#cite_note-32) Harper's collaboration "Boa Sorte/Good Luck" with Brazilian singer [Vanessa da Mata](/wiki/Vanessa_da_Mata "Vanessa da Mata") peaked at No. 1 in Brazil and Portugal. In Brazil it also won a highly coveted [Prêmio Multishow](/wiki/Pr%C3%AAmio_Multishow_de_M%C3%BAsica_Brasileira "Prêmio Multishow de Música Brasileira") for "Best Song" in 2008. Also in 2008, Harper participated in the benefit album *[Songs for Tibet](/wiki/Songs_for_Tibet "Songs for Tibet")*.

Harper discussing *[Lifeline](/wiki/Lifeline_(Ben_Harper_album) "Lifeline (Ben Harper album)")* in 2007

On August 27, 2010, it was reported that Harper had formed a band called [Fistful of Mercy](/wiki/Fistful_of_Mercy "Fistful of Mercy") with [Dhani Harrison](/wiki/Dhani_Harrison "Dhani Harrison") and [Joseph Arthur](/wiki/Joseph_Arthur "Joseph Arthur").[[33]](#cite_note-33) Fistful of Mercy released their debut record, *As I Call You Down*, on October 5, 2010.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

On May 17, 2011, Harper's official site posted that his next album was released, entitled *[Give Till It's Gone](/wiki/Give_Till_It%27s_Gone "Give Till It's Gone")*. The album is a continuation of recording with Relentless7.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

On October 1, 2012, an album from Harper with [Charlie Musselwhite](/wiki/Charlie_Musselwhite "Charlie Musselwhite") was announced titled *[Get Up!](/wiki/Get_Up!_(Ben_Harper_album) "Get Up! (Ben Harper album)")* on Stax Records / Concord Music Group. The official release date for the album was January 29, 2013. *Get Up!* was recorded in Los Angeles and produced by Harper. It is Harper's 12th studio album and first recording since 2011's *Give Till It's Gone* (Virgin).[[36]](#cite_note-36)

In December 2012, it was announced that Harper had co-produced lead vocalist [Natalie Maines](/wiki/Natalie_Maines "Natalie Maines") of the [Dixie Chicks](/wiki/Dixie_Chicks "Dixie Chicks") first solo rock album *[Mother](/wiki/Mother_(Natalie_Maines_album) "Mother (Natalie Maines album)")*.[[37]](#cite_note-37) It was released on May 7, 2013.[[38]](#cite_note-Twitter_Mother_5-7-13-38)

In early 2015, it was announced that Harper and the Innocent Criminals would perform at the [Boston Calling Music Festival](/wiki/Boston_Calling_Music_Festival "Boston Calling Music Festival") in May 2015.[[39]](#cite_note-39)
Ben Harper and the Innocent Criminals performed at Rock Werchter Festival 2015 on June 28, 2015.[[40]](#cite_note-40)

On February 12, 2016, Harper released (as a YouTube video) the single "Call It What It Is", the first track taken from his studio album *[Call It What It Is](/wiki/Call_It_What_It_Is "Call It What It Is")*, released on April 8, 2016.

On April 8, 2019, Harper performed as a guest artist on the seventeenth season of *[American Idol](/wiki/American_Idol "American Idol")* during the second night of the "Top 20 Duets" episode. Harper sang with contestant [Alejandro Aranda](/wiki/Alejandro_Aranda "Alejandro Aranda"), playing "[There Will Be a Light](/wiki/There_Will_Be_a_Light "There Will Be a Light")".[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

At the 40th [Blues Music Awards](/wiki/Blues_Music_Award "Blues Music Award") ceremony, Harper's joint composition with [Charlie Musselwhite](/wiki/Charlie_Musselwhite "Charlie Musselwhite"), "No Mercy In This Land", was named as 'Song of the Year'.[[8]](#cite_note-auto-8)

On March 20, 2019, [Mavis Staples](/wiki/Mavis_Staples "Mavis Staples") announced the album *[We Get By](/wiki/We_Get_By "We Get By")*, featuring eleven tracks written and produced by Harper.[[41]](#cite_note-41) The title track, which features Harper on vocals and in the music video, was released on May 15, 2019.[[42]](#cite_note-youtube1-42)

In 2020, Harper released *[Winter Is for Lovers](/wiki/Winter_Is_for_Lovers "Winter Is for Lovers")*, an album recorded with solo Monteleone lap steel songs. "It started out as an exploration of steel guitar," Harper told *Acoustic Guitar* magazine in a 2020 interview, "*Winter Is for Lovers* is written as one piece of music; let's define that in the way that the old classical guitarists would have, on a single guitar (the Monteleone lap steel).[[43]](#cite_note-43)"

On June 9, 2021, Harper's longtime bassist, collaborator, and Innocent Criminals member, Juan Nelson, died at the age 62. Harper paid tribute to him, stating on Twitter: "Beloved husband, father, musical genius, BHIC band member of 27 years, and the finest man I've ever known. It's near impossible for me to put words to this pain and loss. Rest in Glory our beloved Reverend Juan." No cause of death has been confirmed.

On July 22, 2022, Harper released the album *[Bloodline Maintenance](/wiki/Bloodline_Maintenance "Bloodline Maintenance")*.[[44]](#cite_note-44) The album was dedicated to the memory of Juan Nelson.

On June 2, 2023, Harper released his seventeenth album, *[Wide Open Light](/wiki/Wide_Open_Light "Wide Open Light")*. This is Harper's first album to feature mainly stripped-back acoustic songs with only one track featuring drums.

On April 1, 2024, ex-Innocent Criminals guitarist, Michael Ward died at the age of 57 due to complications from diabetes. On May 1, 2025, Harper released new single, "Before The Rain Dried," in tribute to Ward.[[45]](#cite_note-k350-45)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ben_Harper&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/ca/BenHarperLauraDernDec09.jpg/250px-BenHarperLauraDernDec09.jpg)](/wiki/File:BenHarperLauraDernDec09.jpg)

Harper with his second wife, [Laura Dern](/wiki/Laura_Dern "Laura Dern"), in December 2009

In 1996, Harper married his first wife, Joanna.[[46]](#cite_note-people2002-46) They have two children together.[[46]](#cite_note-people2002-46) Harper and Joanna separated in 2000, and were divorced in 2001.[[46]](#cite_note-people2002-46)

Harper began dating actress [Laura Dern](/wiki/Laura_Dern "Laura Dern") after they met at one of his concerts in Fall 2000.[[46]](#cite_note-people2002-46) Harper and Dern married on December 23, 2005, at their home in Los Angeles.[[47]](#cite_note-47) They have two children,[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) son Ellery Walker (born August 2001)[[46]](#cite_note-people2002-46) and daughter Jaya (born November 2004).[[48]](#cite_note-48) In 2006, the pair co-directed the video for his song "Better Way."[[49]](#cite_note-49) In October 2010, Harper filed for divorce from Dern, citing irreconcilable differences.[[50]](#cite_note-us2012-50) They briefly reconciled and attended the [2012 Golden Globe Awards](/wiki/2012_Golden_Globe_Awards "2012 Golden Globe Awards") together,[[50]](#cite_note-us2012-50) but Dern reactivated the divorce by filing a legal response in July 2012.[[50]](#cite_note-us2012-50) The divorce was finalized in September 2013.[[51]](#cite_note-51)

Harper married social advocate Jaclyn Matfus, on January 1, 2015.[[6]](#cite_note-:1-6) Their son Besso was born in June 2017.[[52]](#cite_note-52)

Harper is a [skateboarder](/wiki/Skateboarding "Skateboarding") who has trained with [Rodney Mullen](/wiki/Rodney_Mullen "Rodney Mullen") and he also owns a warehouse for his personal skateboarding. Harper co-owns the skateboarding brand Roller Horror with professional skateboarder Mike York.[[53]](#cite_note-53) In September 2016, Harper appeared on *The Nine Club* skateboard podcast, hosted by [Chris Roberts](/wiki/Chris_Roberts_(skateboarder) "Chris Roberts (skateboarder)"), where he discussed his relationship and history with skateboarding.[[54]](#cite_note-54) On *The Nine Club*, he also reveals that he went to school and began skateboarding with Chris Miller in [Pomona, California](/wiki/Pomona,_California "Pomona, California").

Harper's cousin is Washington State Supreme Court Justice [Steven Gonzalez](/wiki/Steven_Gonzalez "Steven Gonzalez").[[55]](#cite_note-55)

## Philanthropy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ben_Harper&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Philanthropy")]

Harper supports [Little Kids Rock](/wiki/Little_Kids_Rock "Little Kids Rock"), a national nonprofit organization that works to restore and revitalize music education in disadvantaged U.S. public schools. In 2013, he donated items to their San Francisco Bay Area benefit concert.

On December 11, 2016, he played an acoustic benefit concert at [Bimbos](/wiki/Bimbo%27s_365_Club "Bimbo's 365 Club") in San Francisco supporting New Light India's "Boys Home Project". Founded in 2000 by Urmi Basu, New Light India provides shelter, educational opportunities, recreational facilities, healthcare and legal aid for the girls and women in [Kalighat](/wiki/Kalighat "Kalighat"), notorious for its red light district.

Harper and his wife, Jaclyn, have been long-time supporters[[56]](#cite_note-56) of [International Medical Corps](/wiki/International_Medical_Corps "International Medical Corps")’ work around the world. During the COVID-19 pandemic, they supported the organization's work in California, and Harper recorded a song[[57]](#cite_note-57) to benefit International Medical Corps’ global relief efforts.

## Legacy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ben_Harper&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Legacy")]

A dead-end street has been named after Ben Harper in [Saint-Jean-d'Heurs](/wiki/Saint-Jean-d%27Heurs "Saint-Jean-d'Heurs"), a rural commune of France.[[58]](#cite_note-58)
